message:
fix(server): eliminate per-worker async runtimes, use saturating_sub, harden probes

Remove unnecessary single-thread async runtime construction in ack_ttl,
cleanup, retention, and tool_metrics background workers. These workers
only use synchronous DB calls, so the extra runtime was pure overhead
and a source of thread-pool exhaustion under load.

Replace all .lock().unwrap() on background worker mutexes with
.unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) to recover from poisoned locks
instead of panicking, keeping the server alive after thread panics.

Convert recursive dir_size(), count_md_files_recursive(), and
count_old_files_recursive() in retention.rs to iterative stack-based
traversal to avoid stack overflow on deeply nested storage trees.

Use saturating_sub for all timestamp arithmetic in cleanup.rs to
prevent underflow on clock skew.

Tighten HTTP health probe in lib.rs to read_exact 12 bytes instead of
a variable-length read, avoiding false negatives from partial reads.

Add header size limit (8KB) to startup_checks port-conflict detection
to prevent unbounded memory growth if a non-HTTP service responds with
garbage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
